Test Description: Default reset-value verification and masked write/read verification across 49 GPIO-related registers using read/write masks and expected default values.
Remarks: Notes from source: skip_array comment mentions SKIPPING VRRW registers; additional note warns that forcing DIN low affects level select causing default read mismatch if not handled.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point: int test_case()
1) Call chk_rst_val()
   - Loop i = 0..48:
     * addr = addr_array[i]
     * If skip_rst_array[i] == 1: continue
     * If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue
     * READ: data_rd = read_reg(addr)
     * COMPUTE: data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE)
     * COMPARE: if data == default_value_array[i] then pass else { def_fail_cnt++ ; print failure }
2) Call chk_rd_wr()
   - Define chk_val[6] = {0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000}
   - For each j in 0..5:
     * data_wr = chk_val[j]
     * WRITE PHASE: loop i = 0..48:
       - addr = addr_array[i]
       - If skip_array[i] == 1: continue
       - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue
       - WRITE: write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i]))
     * READ/VERIFY PHASE: loop i = 0..48:
       - addr = addr_array[i]
       - If skip_array[i] == 1: continue
       - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue
       - If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue
       - READ: data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i])
       - COMPUTE: wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF)
       - COMPUTE: exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]))
       - COMPARE: if data_rd == exp_val then pass else { wr_fail_cnt++ ; print mismatch }
3) Terminate: if (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1) else finish(0)
Timing: no delay/wait functions executed in active paths.
Entry/Helper order: test_case -> chk_rst_val -> chk_rd_wr -> finish.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
Default check: pass when (read_reg(addr_array[i]) & 0xFFFFFFFE) equals default_value_array[i] for readable and non-skipped registers; else def_fail_cnt increments.
Write/read check: for writable and readable and non-skipped registers, pass when (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]) equals computed exp_val as defined in source; else wr_fail_cnt increments.
Overall pass condition: finish(0) only when def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0; otherwise finish(1).

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and verification for GPIO[8..39] with per-pin configuration
Code Generation (Required/Not): Arm wait before edge to avoid race; bounded wait with timeout to prevent infinite loop.
column14:
Entry point: int test_case()
1) test_err = 0
2) Conditionally enable GIC IRQ (GPIO0 -> 87, GPIO1 -> 88)
3) Enable sysreg interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIOx_INTR)
4) Drive pads to known high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF)
5) Configure per-pin negedge input and clear: for i=0..31:
   - addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4)
   - write_reg(addr1, (1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16)) ; wait_on(10)
6) For each pin i=0..31:
   - wr_val = 1u<<i
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val)
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val) ; wait_on(10)
   - int_pend = 1
   - write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF) ; wait_on(30) ; write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val)
   - timeout=5000; while(int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10)
   - if timeout==0: printf timeout error; test_err++
7) finish(test_err)
ISR: void Default_IRQHandler()
   - local_wr = 1u<<i; int_pend=0
   - write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF)
   - raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); rdata = read_reg(raddr)
   - If (rdata & 0x1) != 0: test_err++
   - If (rdata & 0x2) != 0x0 then:
       * rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
       * If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) test_err++
       * write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20) | (1<<16))
       * write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr)
       * rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0x0) test_err++
       * write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIOx_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87/88)
     else: test_err++
Timing: wait_on(10/30) and bounded polling with timeout=5000.
column16:
Runtime timeout check: pass if int_pend cleared before timeout; fail if timeout expires.
Per-pin DIN validation: pass if (rdata & 0x1) == 0 after negedge; fail otherwise.
Per-pin raw bit: pass if (rdata & 0x2) != 0; fail otherwise.
Group status set: pass if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & (1u<<i)) != 0; fail otherwise.
Group clear: after clearing, pass if read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0; fail otherwise.
Sysreg clear: after RAW_STCR1 write and read-back, pass if respective GPIOx bit is cleared; fail otherwise.
Overall: pass when test_err == 0 at finish(test_err); otherwise fail.

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable and verification for GPIO[8..39] with per-pin configuration
Code Generation (Required/Not): Arm before edge and use bounded wait to avoid hangs.
column14:
Entry point: void test_case()
1) Optionally enable GIC IRQ (87/88 based on GPIOx)
2) test_err = 0
3) Enable sysreg interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIOx_INTR)
4) For i=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4, 0x00020000) // posedge enable
5) wait_on(10)
6) Configure input mode groups:
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, 0x000000FF)
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, 0x000000FF)
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, 0x000000FF)
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, 0x000000FF)
7) wait_on(10)
8) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
9) For i=0..31:
   - write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10); int_pend = 1; write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF)
   - timeout=2000; while ((int_pend==1) && (--timeout>0)) wait_on(10)
   - if timeout==0: printf timeout error; test_err++; break
   - write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10)
10) finish(test_err)
ISR: void Default_IRQHandler()
   - wr_val = 1<<i; int_pend = 0
   - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000)
   - If (rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0 then pass else { printf error; test_err++ }
   - For j=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4, 0x00010000)
   - wait_on(2)
   - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp == 0x0) pass else { printf error; test_err++ }
   - If GPIO0: write_reg/read_reg MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1; check bit cleared; else if GPIO1: same for GPIO1 bit
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
   - GIC_ClearIRQ(87/88)
Timing: multiple wait_on() and bounded loops with timeout=2000.
column16:
Runtime timeout check: pass if int_pend cleared before timeout; fail on timeout.
Group interrupt presence: pass if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0; fail otherwise.
Group clear: after per-pin clear and wait_on(2), pass if subsequent read equals 0x0; fail otherwise.
Sysreg clear: after RAW_STCR1 write and read-back, pass if respective bit is cleared; fail otherwise.
Overall: pass when test_err == 0 at finish(test_err); otherwise fail.
